metadata.name: 3-minute Psychomotor Vigilance Task (PVT)
metadata.author: Cog Task Engine — adapted from Benderoth et al. (2021)
metadata.notes: Reproduces the 3-min PVT as described in Benderoth, Hoermann, Schiessl & Elmenhorst (2021), 'Reliability and validity of a 3-min psychomotor vigilance task in assessing sensitivity to sleep loss and alcohol', SLEEP 44(11), doi:10.1093/sleep/zsab151. Original spec: green LED on a handheld device, ISI random 1-5 s, single button response, RT displayed for 1 s post-response, 3-min total. Engine adaptations: (1) RT timestamps are frame-quantized (~16.7 ms at 60 Hz) rather than the hardware sub-ms — well below the paper's reported sleep-loss/alcohol effect sizes (50-200 ms). (2) The engine has no template substitution in feedback text, so post-response feedback is a static 'Responded' message instead of the numeric RT. The precise per-trial RT remains in the payload's trial.response_rt_us field. (3) The paper's lapse threshold (>=355 ms) and false-start threshold (<=130 ms) are NOT enforced at the engine level — both filters are downstream-analyst responsibility against the captured RT. (4) The paper's session is time-bounded (180 s); the engine is trial-bounded, so n_trials=42 produces approximately 3 min at the configured timing (mean ISI 3 s + mean RT ~0.3-0.4 s + 1 s feedback ~ 4.4 s/trial x 42 ~ 184 s). (5) Pre-stimulus presses during ITI are silently dropped by the engine instead of being recorded as 'false starts' — a minor divergence from the original.

instructions.text:
Watch for the GREEN DOT.

When you see it, press SPACE (or tap TAP) as fast as you can.

Do NOT press before the dot appears.

The task takes about 3 minutes.
